heading: Presumption of sound condition
label: 1111 Presumption of sound condition
text: For the purposes of , every veteran shall be taken to have been in sound condition when examined, accepted, and enrolled for service, except as to defects, infirmities, or disorders noted at the time of the examination, acceptance, and enrollment, or where clear and unmistakable evidence demonstrates that the injury or disease existed before acceptance and enrollment and was not aggravated by such service.section 1110 of this title
notes: Amendments1991—, renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 102–83, § 5(a)section 311 of this title, substituted “1110” for “310”.Pub. L. 102–83, § 5(c)(1)
amendments: Amendments1991—, renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 102–83, § 5(a)section 311 of this title, substituted “1110” for “310”.Pub. L. 102–83, § 5(c)(1)
